title: Phase 2: PWA — chat + browse/edit + capture
summary: Ship a SvelteKit PWA powered by a custom @anthropic-ai/sdk harness with three modes against the brain: a chat agent with brain-aware tools, a graph browser/editor that lets the human directly view and fix brain data, and a single-purpose capture screen.
outOfScope: Async/queued write path (Phase 3 — Phase 2 captures are synchronous)
Backups, dedup audit, fallback memory layer (Phase 4)
technicalConsiderations: Pre-phase decision pinned: SvelteKit (lighter footprint, single Node process for UI + API). Switch to Next.js only if a specific component/auth library forces it
New top-level pwa/ project; new compose service pwa; new Caddy vhost app.{domain}
No second store. Graphiti (FalkorDB) is the only persistent service. Tool calls log to stderr (docker logs pwa); captures and add_episode tool calls block synchronously on Graphiti's 1–3s extraction

Graph browser/editor is the surface that makes graph-canonical viable: read APIs over Graphiti + a six-mutation editor (rename_entity, set_entity_attribute, delete_entity, delete_edge, merge_entities, update_episode_body) + browse/entity/episode UI with inline edit
Cookie auth path on app.{domain} for phone UX (bearer retained for API)
Risks: streaming + tool-call buffering, mutation API granularity (some operations may need to be synthesized from Graphiti primitives), mobile UX for the merge-entities modal
